Today is February 13, 2026. As the Munich Security Conference begins, a group of former US officials issued an open letter regarding the importance of Nato. According to the source material, how many former US ambassadors to Nato and former American supreme commanders in Europe signed this letter?
(A) Eight former ambassadors and eight former supreme commanders
(B) Fifty world leaders and eight former ambassadors
(C) Ten former ambassadors and ten former supreme commanders
(D) Eight former ambassadors and five former supreme commanders
(E) Twelve former ambassadors and eight former supreme commanders
explanation: The article states that "eight former US ambassadors to Nato and eight former American supreme commanders in Europe issued an open letter calling for Washington to maintain its support for the Western defensive alliance."
model_output:
Based on the web search results, the open letter was signed by eight former US ambassadors to NATO and eight former American supreme commanders in Europe[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c99jxryd9xko)[[2]](https://lavocedinewyork.com/en/news/2026/02/13/rubio-in-munich-as-u-s-europe-divided-over-security-ukraine).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c99jxryd9xko])
- La Voce di New York ([https://lavocedinewyork.com/en/news/2026/02/13/rubio-in-munich-as-u-s-europe-divided-over-security-ukraine])

Today is February 13, 2026. Kathryn Ruemmler, the top lawyer at Goldman Sachs, has resigned following disclosures about her relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. According to emails cited in the report, what was the specific value of the Bergdorf Goodman gift cards Epstein gave to Ruemmler?
(A) $7,300
(B) $10,000
(C) $2,000
(D) $1,000
(E) $5,000
explanation: The article explicitly mentions that "the emails also show that Epstein lavished Ruemmler with gifts, including flowers, wine, a Hermès bag, $10,000 (£7,300) in Bergdorf Goodman gift cards and an Apple watch."
model_output:
Based on web search results and detailed page content from multiple news sources dated February 13, 2026, the emails cited in reports about Kathryn Ruemmler's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein indicate that he gave her $10,000 in Bergdorf Goodman gift cards[[1]](https://www.bloomberg.com/opinion/articles/2026-02-13/goldman-sachs-s-kathryn-ruemmler-worn-out-her-welcome)[[2]](https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/top-goldman-sachs-lawyer-who-called-epstein-uncle-jeffrey-resigns/ar-AA1WiH9j)[[3]](https://www.nonbillable.co.uk/news/goldman-sachs-chief-lawyer-resign-epstein-latham-watkins-kathy-ruemmler). Other gift values mentioned in various reports (such as a Hermès handbag ranging from $9,350 to $9,400, or a Fendi coat and bag at $6,800) do not match the specific Bergdorf Goodman gift cards in question[[4]](https://www.reuters.com/legal/legalindustry/jeffrey-epstein-gave-her-9350-handbag-did-goldman-sachs-departing-top-lawyer-2026-02-13)[[3]](https://www.nonbillable.co.uk/news/goldman-sachs-chief-lawyer-resign-epstein-latham-watkins-kathy-ruemmler).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Bloomberg (https://www.bloomberg.com/opinion/articles/2026-02-13/goldman-sachs-s-kathryn-ruemmler-worn-out-her-welcome)
- MSN (https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/world/top-goldman-sachs-lawyer-who-called-epstein-uncle-jeffrey-resigns/ar-AA1WiH9j)
- Nonbillable (https://www.nonbillable.co.uk/news/goldman-sachs-chief-lawyer-resign-epstein-latham-watkins-kathy-ruemmler)

Today is February 13, 2026. The US Department of Labor recently released inflation data for January. While some prices rose, others saw significant declines. By what percentage did the cost of eggs fall compared to January 2025?
(A) 13%
(B) 2.4%
(C) 34%
(D) 2.7%
(E) 1.5%
explanation: The article states: "Meanwhile, the cost of eggs fell more than 34% from January 2025."
model_output:
Based on the latest CPI data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics for January 2026, the year-over-year change in egg prices shows a decline of 34.2% from January 2025[[1]](https://www.bls.gov/news.release/cpi.t02.htm). This is likely rounded to 34% in the provided options, which aligns closely with the reported figure and is far more consistent than the other choices (which appear to reflect overall inflation rates or other unrelated metrics).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI Table] (https://www.bls.gov/news.release/cpi.t02.htm)
- [Fox Business] (https://www.foxbusiness.com/economy/cpi-inflation-january-2026)
- [YCharts] (https://ycharts.com/indicators/us_consumer_price_index_eggs)

</final_answer>

Today is February 13, 2026. The Trump administration is suing Harvard University over its admissions data. According to the report, how much in research grants did President Trump revoke from Harvard in April of last year?
(A) Around $2 billion
(B) Exactly $1 billion
(C) $730 million
(D) $2 million
(E) $500 million
explanation: The article states: "In April last year, Trump revoked around $2bn in research grants to Harvard and froze federal funding."
model_output:
The scenario described appears to be part of an ongoing dispute between the Trump administration and Harvard University, involving issues like admissions practices, alleged discrimination, and prior conflicts over campus policies (e.g., antisemitism allegations). Based on recent reports from today (February 13, 2026), the lawsuit filed by the administration seeks to compel Harvard to provide admissions data related to race and ethnicity considerations, but it does not allege discrimination or seek damages/funding cuts in this specific suit.

However, these reports reference a prior action where research grants were revoked. Specifically, in April 2025, the administration revoked around $2 billion in research grants from Harvard (along with freezing other federal funding), amid escalating tensions. A court later overturned this, but the White House challenged the ruling. This amount aligns with details in multiple sources covering the backstory.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clygnv7z8jxo)[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/clygnv7z8jxo)
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crkryely657o)[[2]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/crkryely657o)

Today is February 13, 2026. A mass shooting has occurred in the small Canadian town of Tumbler Ridge. According to the police reports cited in the article, how old was the suspect, Jesse Van Rootselaar?
(A) 17 years old
(B) 12 years old
(C) 21 years old
(D) 18 years old
(E) 16 years old
explanation: The article states: "...investigators still trying to determine why 18-year-old suspect Jesse Van Rootselaar carried out the attack."
model_output:
Based on web search results and details extracted from a specific news article, the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, appears to have occurred on or around February 11-12, 2026 (with reports emerging shortly thereafter). Multiple sources, including direct references to statements from the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP), identify the suspect as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar.[[1]](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/suspect-in-canada-school-shooting-is-identified-as-18-year-old-who-had-prior-mental-health-calls)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GTPuCJw7uaM)[[3]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/12/canada-police-identify-shooting-suspect-as-18-year-old-jesse-van-rootselaar) For example, RCMP Deputy Commissioner Dwayne McDonald is cited in police reports as confirming the suspect's age and noting prior mental health interactions with authorities.[[1]](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/suspect-in-canada-school-shooting-is-identified-as-18-year-old-who-had-prior-mental-health-calls) No sources indicated any other age.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- PBS NewsHour (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/suspect-in-canada-school-shooting-is-identified-as-18-year-old-who-had-prior-mental-health-calls)
- BBC News (https://www.bbc.com/news/live/cr5lnzqdr5pt)
- Al Jazeera (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/12/canada-police-identify-shooting-suspect-as-18-year-old-jesse-van-rootselaar)

model_output:

This is tied directly to coverage of her opposition to the "Swift Home" trademark by Cathay Home.

No sources indicate an exact number (e.g., 730), a lower approximation (e.g., around 50), or a higher one (e.g., more than 500). "More than 100" is technically true but less precise than the sources' phrasing.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 13, 2026. A new report from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York analyzes the impact of Trump's 2025 tariffs. According to the research, what was the average tariff rate on imported goods at the start of 2025?
(A) 13%
(B) 2.6%
(C) 9.9%
(D) 90%
(E) 100%
explanation: The article states: "...in 2025, the average tariff rate on imported goods rose to 13% from just 2.6% at the start of the year."
model_output:
The question asks for the average tariff rate on imported goods at the start of 2025, according to a new 2026 report from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York analyzing the impact of Trump's 2025 tariffs.

Based on my web search, multiple sources discussing this report consistently indicate that the average tariff rate increased from 2.6% at the beginning of 2025 to 13% by the end of the year[[1]](https://libertystreeteconomics.newyorkfed.org/2026/02/who-is-paying-for-the-2025-u-s-tariffs)[[2]](https://fortune.com/2026/02/13/new-york-fed-economists-confirm-americans-footing-trump-tariff-bill)[[3]](https://english.news.cn/northamerica/20260213/2a6be9fb0c06409297fd991b2f8c927e/c.html). This matches option (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 13, 2026. Despite economic growth, the US labor market has seen a slowdown in hiring. According to the source material, what was the average number of jobs added per month in the US last year?
(A) 150,000
(B) 42,000
(C) 15,000
(D) 1,200
(E) 2,000
explanation: The article states: "Last year the US added an average of just 15,000 jobs a month, very few by historic standards."
model_output:
The question asks for the average number of monthly job additions in the US during 2025, based on source material (which, given the date of February 13, 2026, would reflect the latest revised labor market data released a couple of days prior). Based on recent reports from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (as covered in news sources), the total job additions for 2025 were revised downward to 181,000, which equates to an average of 15,000 jobs per month[[1]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/11/economy/us-jobs-report-january-final)[[2]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y5xjzpr52o)[[3]](https://www.wuft.org/2026-02-11/u-s-unexpectedly-adds-130-000-jobs-in-january-after-a-weak-2025). This reflects a significant slowdown in hiring despite broader economic growth, aligning with the query's description.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 13, 2026. The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is facing a funding gap. According to the article, what is the name of the legislation from last year that provided $165bn to the DHS and $75bn specifically for ICE?
(A) The SAVE America Act
(B) The Inflation Reduction Act
(C) The Clean Air Act
(D) The One Big Beautiful Bill Act
(E) The DHS Funding Act
explanation: The article states: "DHS has $165bn (£120bn) from Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act - which also included $75bn to fund ICE..."
model_output:
First, the question refers to "the article," but none is provided in the query. Based on the date (February 13, 2026) and details, I used web search tools to identify relevant recent articles about a DHS funding gap in 2026, tied to 2025 legislation providing $165 billion to DHS and $75 billion to ICE.

Key findings:
- Multiple sources reference the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act" (often abbreviated OBBBA or BBB) signed into law in July 2025.
- It provided $165 billion total to DHS.
- It specifically allocated $75 billion to ICE (e.g., for hiring agents, expanding detention, etc.).
- This matches the funding amounts in the question, and current (2026) articles discuss a DHS funding gap amid debates over extending or adding to this prior funding.

This aligns with option (D). No other options match the details from the sources.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Homeland Security] (https://www.dhs.gov/news/2025/07/04/secretary-noem-commends-president-trump-and-one-big-beautiful-bill-signing-law)[[1]](https://www.dhs.gov/news/2025/07/04/secretary-noem-commends-president-trump-and-one-big-beautiful-bill-signing-law)
- [House Appropriations Democrats] (http://democrats-appropriations.house.gov/news/press-releases/appropriations-committees-release-homeland-security-funding-bill)[[2]](http://democrats-appropriations.house.gov/news/press-releases/appropriations-committees-release-homeland-security-funding-bill)

Today is February 13, 2026. Police have provided a timeline for the shooting in Tumbler Ridge. At approximately what local time did police respond to reports of a shooting at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School on Tuesday?
(A) 21:30
(B) 13:30
(C) 12:00
(D) 15:00
(E) 09:00
explanation: The article states: "...police responded to reports of a shooting at around 13:30 local time (21:30 GMT) on Tuesday."
model_output:
The various sources show some inconsistency on the exact time and time zone, likely due to reporting differences between Pacific and Mountain Time. However, the official RCMP update and multiple news timelines (including Global News, CBC, and Wikipedia) point to police receiving and responding to the initial reports at approximately 1:20 p.m. PST (which is 2:20 p.m. local MST). Some sources (e.g., BBC and The Guardian) list it as around 1:20 p.m. or 1:30 p.m. local time, possibly treating PST as local by error. Given the "approximately" wording and the options provided, this aligns closest with 13:30.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [RCMP] (https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350208)[[1]](https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350208)
- [Global News] (https://globalnews.ca/news/11662692/tumbler-ridge-school-shooting-timeline)[[2]](https://globalnews.ca/news/11662692/tumbler-ridge-school-shooting-timeline)

(D) 4.4%
(E) 4.3%
explanation: The article states: "Meanwhile, the wider economy continues to grow, expanding at a robust annual pace of 4.4% in the most recent figures."
model_output:
The most recent actual (not projected) US GDP growth rate available as of February 13, 2026, is the finalized figure for Q3 2025, which is an annualized 4.4%. This matches the description of the wider economy showing growth despite labor market challenges, and it appears in multiple recent articles as the latest cited figure (with Q4 2025 data not yet released until later in February). Other options like 4.3% appear to relate to either initial estimates (later revised) or unrelated metrics like the unemployment rate.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>
